title: Norn as Primary Runtime
intention: Norn becomes the primary runtime for all member-facing work in Meridian. Every DM, wake prompt, inbox triage, and interactive assistant session runs through Norn in-process within the server. Claude Runner stays only for legacy background task dispatch until it can be retired entirely. Member-facing sessions run as typed, observable, in-process agent loops rather than opaque subprocess invocations. Session state persists across visits without relying on a CLI binary's internal persistence. The model sees a focused set of domain tools instead of a raw shell with 175 CLI commands. This is the June 15 deadline work.
problem: Session persistence is missing in-process. make_norn_step_runner builds an AgentBuilder without calling .session(), creating a bare EventStore::new() — in-memory only, no JsonlSink, events dropped when the step finishes. The server needs the same durability as norn-cli but with server-managed paths and PG telemetry integration.

No Meridian domain tools. Norn has 16 standard developer tools but an agent running as a Meridian member needs messaging, status management, workflow dispatch, VCS operations, and project management. The 175+ CLI commands across 26 groups cannot be exposed as flat tools — the design needs hierarchical namespace tools.

No assistant service integration. The assistant service is built around ClaudeProcess — subprocess via ClaudeCommand, JSONL on stdin/stdout, TranslateState converting ClaudeEvent to AssistantEvent. Norn is in-process with typed AgentEvent broadcast. The session loop, event translation, persistence pipeline, Redis streaming, WebSocket delivery, and wake prompt mechanism all assume the subprocess model.

solution.summary: Branch AssistantService on RuntimeKind to spawn Norn sessions alongside Claude Runner. Norn sessions run as async tokio::spawn tasks, produce the same AssistantEvent stream via NornTranslateState, persist to both JSONL (session resume) and PG (UI history), and expose Meridian capabilities as 7 namespace tools with a command discriminant. The transition is incremental — both runtimes coexist with profile-based selection.

pieces.title: Runtime discriminant and session lifecycle
pieces.description: RuntimeKind enum (ClaudeRunner | Norn) on SessionHandle. AssistantService::start_session branches after token acquisition. Both paths produce broadcast::Sender<AssistantEvent>. spawn_norn_loop builds AgentBuilder, creates event channels, spawns async task. Existing Claude Runner path unchanged.

pieces.title: Norn session loop and event translation
pieces.description: Async run_norn_session_loop multiplexes AgentEvent broadcast, SessionCommand mpsc, idle timeout, and agent completion via tokio::select. NornTranslateState converts ProviderEvent to AssistantEvent: TextDelta, ThinkingDelta, ToolUseStarted, ToolResult, Usage+TurnComplete, SubAgentEvent. Synthesizes SessionStarted from AgentBuilder config.

pieces.title: Session persistence — JSONL and PG
pieces.description: Server-managed JSONL at {data_dir}/sessions/norn/{session_id}.jsonl via EventStore+JsonlSink. Resume reads JSONL, creates EventStore::with_sink_and_events. Fork copies source JSONL to new path. PG persistence and Redis streaming continue unchanged — they subscribe to AssistantEvent broadcast.

pieces.title: Wake config and authentication
pieces.description: wake_configs table gains runtime column (nullable, default NULL = server default). Token acquisition from same TokenPool as Claude Runner. Wake prompt delivery via AgentBuilder::prompt() for Norn, SessionConfig::initial_message for Claude Runner. WakeupService unchanged.

pieces.title: Namespace tool infrastructure and critical tools
pieces.description: MeridianToolContext holds Arc<ServiceContext>. CallerContext carries member_id, session_id (Uuid), workspace_id (Option<Uuid>), agent_cert_fingerprint (Option<[u8;32]>). register_meridian_tools conditionally registers tools based on profile. MessagingTool (12 commands), MemberTool (7), WorkspaceTool (4) implement norn::tool::Tool with command discriminant.

pieces.title: Development and workflow namespace tools
pieces.description: SourceTool (25 commands), BranchTool (22 commands), WorkflowTool (14 commands including scheduler). All use command discriminant. Profile-gated registration. Service errors return ToolOutput with is_error=true.

pieces.title: In-process notification injection
pieces.description: Post-tool-batch notification check queries pending notifications via MessagingService, formats as ChannelMessage with DeliveryMode::Steer, injects via InboundSender. Replaces CLI subprocess hook. Notifications marked as delivered after injection to prevent re-injection.

pieces.title: Frontend runtime awareness
pieces.description: runtime_kind field surfaced in frontend session types and hooks. Model fallback strings in AssistantPanel.tsx become runtime-aware. Session toolbar gains runtime badge (Norn or Claude Runner) with distinct visual treatment.

decisions.title: RuntimeKind discriminant per session
decisions.body: A RuntimeKind enum on SessionHandle distinguishes Norn from Claude Runner. AssistantService::start_session checks configuration to select the runtime. Both paths produce the same broadcast::Sender<AssistantEvent>, so downstream consumers are runtime-agnostic.
decisions.choice: RuntimeKind enum with ClaudeRunner (default) and Norn variants.
decisions.rationale: Clean branching point. Both paths share the session registry, command channel, and event broadcast. The enum is the single place where runtime-specific logic lives.
implications: SessionHandle stores RuntimeKind for lifecycle queries
SessionConfig gains optional runtime field
Server config has default_runtime setting

decisions.title: Dual persistence — JSONL and PG
decisions.body: Two independent persistence paths serve different consumers. JSONL files are Norn's source of truth for session resume (EventStore write-through). PG assistant_session_events are the UI's source of truth for history display. They are complementary, not redundant.
decisions.choice: JSONL for Norn resume, PG for UI history. Independent streams.
decisions.rationale: JSONL carries Norn's internal state (conversation threading, cache keys). PG carries the UI's event stream (text deltas, tool summaries, turn boundaries). Conflating them loses fidelity in both directions.
implications: Server-managed session directory at {data_dir}/sessions/norn/
Resume reads JSONL, creates EventStore::with_sink_and_events
PG persistence subscribes to AssistantEvent broadcast, unchanged

decisions.title: Async Norn session loop via tokio::spawn
decisions.body: Norn is async. The session loop runs as a tokio::spawn task, multiplexing four sources via tokio::select: AgentEvent broadcast, SessionCommand mpsc, idle timeout, and agent completion (JoinHandle).
decisions.choice: tokio::spawn, not spawn_blocking. Four-source select loop.
decisions.rationale: Norn's Agent::run() is async. spawn_blocking would needlessly waste a thread pool slot. The four-source select matches the Claude Runner loop's responsibilities (events, commands, timeout, completion) with async semantics.
implications: No crossbeam bridges needed (async channels throughout)
Idle timeout resets on AgentEvent, not on wall clock
CancellationToken for Stop/Kill instead of process signals

decisions.title: NornTranslateState parallel to TranslateState
decisions.body: NornTranslateState converts AgentEvent/ProviderEvent to AssistantEvent, parallel to the existing TranslateState that converts ClaudeEvent to AssistantEvent. Key mappings: TextDelta→TextDelta, ThinkingDelta→ThinkingDelta, ToolCallComplete→ToolUseStarted, Done→Usage+TurnComplete.
decisions.rationale: The input types are fundamentally different (ProviderEvent vs ClaudeEvent). A shared translator would be a forced abstraction. The output type (AssistantEvent) is the shared interface that matters.
implications: Per-turn state (tool IDs, sub-agents, block index) cleared on Done
Sub-agent events wrapped in AssistantEvent::SubAgentEvent
SessionStarted synthesized from AgentBuilder config

decisions.title: Hierarchical namespace tools with command discriminant
decisions.body: 175 flat tools would overwhelm the model. Meridian capabilities are grouped into namespace tools — each namespace is a single Norn Tool with a command discriminant. Nine tools defined, eight active (project deferred). Three-tool split per Tom's direction: meridian_member (identity), meridian_workspace (environment), meridian_exchange (cross-instance).
decisions.choice: command discriminant, not action (avoids ToolEffect overloading). Nine tools, eight active.
decisions.rationale: Model sees 5-7 tools instead of 175. Each namespace has focused description. Profile gating at namespace level. Adding a CLI command = adding a command variant, not a new tool.
implications: meridian_messaging (12), meridian_member (7), meridian_workspace (4), meridian_source (25), meridian_branch (22), meridian_workflow (14), meridian_exchange (21)
meridian_review (10) defined by tool-architecture cluster
meridian_project deferred

decisions.title: Profile-gated tool sets with bash opt-in
decisions.body: Agent profiles control which namespace tools are available. Default profiles omit bash entirely — agents operate through typed namespace tools plus file tools. Bash is opt-in for dev agents that run tests and builds.
decisions.choice: Bash opt-in. Default profiles are bash-free.
decisions.rationale: Bash is the escape hatch that makes every other security measure advisory. Certificate auth, contract capabilities, per-tool effect classification — none matter if the agent can shell out. Bash-free profiles are genuinely secure.
implications: Member agent: messaging + member + workflow (no bash)
Dev agent: source + branch + bash
register_meridian_tools checks profile tool list

decisions.title: Direct service layer calls, no HTTP
decisions.body: Namespace tools call service-layer functions directly via ServiceContext. CallerContext carries member_id, session_id (Uuid), workspace_id (Option<Uuid>), agent_cert_fingerprint (Option<[u8;32]>). Published as Arc extension on ToolContext at agent construction. Unforgeable.
decisions.choice: In-process calls via ServiceContext. CallerContext canonical definition in NR-006 R2.
decisions.rationale: Norn runs in the same process as the services. HTTP round-trips are unnecessary overhead. CallerContext as an unforgeable extension eliminates identity spoofing.
implications: tool-architecture cluster references NR-006 R2 for CallerContext
VM sessions use subset ServiceContext (workflow + source only)
Unavailable services return context-aware error messages

decisions.title: Wake prompt unchanged, delivery mechanism changes
decisions.body: build_wake_prompt is runtime-agnostic — it fetches inbox, formats DM envelopes, returns text. For Norn, the text is passed to AgentBuilder::prompt(). For Claude Runner, it remains SessionConfig::initial_message. WakeupService calls start_session which branches on RuntimeKind.
decisions.rationale: The prompt format works. Changing delivery is sufficient — changing format would be unnecessary churn.
implications: WakeupService unchanged beyond reading resolved RuntimeKind
wake_configs table gains runtime column for per-member override

decisions.title: Execution context dispatch for host and VM
decisions.body: Both host and VM provide in-process service calls. The host ServiceContext has the full service set. The VM ServiceContext has only workflow + source. Namespace tools call whichever ServiceContext they receive. When a tool calls an unavailable service, the interface returns a context-aware error.
decisions.choice: Same in-process pattern, different service availability. VM agents use profile omission for unavailable tools.
decisions.rationale: Per Steve's insight: don't register tools for unavailable services, just omit from VM profile. The agent never sees them and can't call them. Cleaner than register-then-error.
implications: VM profile omits meridian_messaging, meridian_member, meridian_workspace
exchange-workspaces D6 wires VM's ServiceContext into register_meridian_tools

decisions.title: CallerContext with agent certificate fingerprint
decisions.body: CallerContext gains agent_cert_fingerprint: Option<[u8; 32]> for exchange/VM identity. None for host-instance sessions, Some for VM/exchange sessions. SHA-256 hash matching the exchange crate's representation.
decisions.rationale: Namespace tools that cross the exchange boundary need to sign with the agent cert. The fingerprint identifies which cert to use.
implications: meridian-trust crate provides cert verification
meridian_exchange tool gated by profile

decisions.title: Workspace focus for shared workspace access via virtiofs
decisions.body: Agents on the home instance access shared VM workspace files through the virtiofs mount. meridian_source working directory resolution supports a workspace focus concept — when focused on a shared workspace, source operations target the mount path. The agent stays local with full services but targets the VM's files.
decisions.choice: Workspace focus routes source operations to virtiofs mount path.
decisions.rationale: Agent keeps full service access (messaging, member) while operating on remote files. Avoids running the agent in the VM with limited services.
implications: exchange-workspaces D20 specifies workspace_focus/unfocus commands
meridian_source working directory resolution must support mount paths
Session metadata (CallerContext.workspace_id + workspace mode) drives resolution

decisions.title: Fix make_norn_step_runner persistence
decisions.body: make_norn_step_runner must call .session(store) on the AgentBuilder. Session file path derived from workflow execution step context: {data_dir}/sessions/workflow/{step_id}.jsonl.
decisions.rationale: Without this, workflow step events are lost when the step completes.
implications: Separate brief (NR-008) because it touches workflow path, not assistant path

decisions.title: In-process notification injection via InboundChannel
decisions.body: Replace the CLI subprocess notification hook with an in-process post-tool-batch check. After each tool batch completes, query pending notifications via MessagingService, format as ChannelMessage with DeliveryMode::Steer, inject via InboundSender. The agent picks them up at the next drain point — same mechanism as mid-session DM delivery.
decisions.choice: InboundChannel injection at tool batch boundary, not tool lifecycle hooks.
decisions.rationale: InboundChannel is already proven for DM delivery. The three tools with lifecycle hooks (Edit, Patch, Write) are file mutation tools — wrong place for notifications. The tool batch boundary matches the existing PreToolUse hook timing.
implications: Two new service wrapper methods: get_pending_notifications, count_pending_notifications
NotificationChecker struct constructed per-session from ServiceContext and CallerContext
Notifications marked delivered after injection to prevent re-injection

decisions.title: Frontend runtime awareness via existing API field
decisions.body: The backend SessionInfoResponse already includes runtime_kind. The frontend never reads it. Surface runtime_kind through SessionStartedEvent and assistant hooks. Fix two hardcoded 'claude' model fallbacks in AssistantPanel.tsx. Add runtime badge to session toolbar.
decisions.choice: Read existing backend field, don't create new infrastructure.
decisions.rationale: The data path exists. The gap is purely frontend consumption.
implications: SessionStartedEvent gains optional runtime_kind field
Model fallback becomes runtime-aware (Norn vs Claude Runner)
Session toolbar shows runtime badge with distinct color

constraints.description: No new external dependencies. Namespace tools call service-layer functions via ServiceContext, not HTTP.
AssistantEvent is the single event type for all downstream consumers. Both runtimes produce it.
JSONL session files are Norn's source of truth for resume. PG is the UI's source of truth. Neither replaces the other.
The model sees at most 25 tools (16 standard Norn + up to 7 namespace + 2 optional web). Profile filtering reduces this in practice.
Session IDs are UUIDs shared between the JSONL filename, PG assistant_session_events key, and SessionHandle registry key.
Wake prompt delivery mechanism changes, format does not.
mod.rs contains only pub mod declarations and re-exports.
No file over 500 lines of code (excluding tests, comments, whitespace).
All errors handled via thiserror in library code. No .unwrap() or .expect() in library code.
Namespace tools implement the existing Tool trait. No changes to the trait interface.

goals: Member-facing sessions run through Norn in-process with full session persistence and resume capability
The model sees 5-7 focused namespace tools for Meridian operations, not 175 flat commands
PG persistence, Redis streaming, and WebSocket delivery work identically for Norn and Claude Runner sessions
Wake prompts and inbox delivery work through Norn without changes to the wake prompt format
Agent profiles control which namespace tools are available, matching the agent's role
The transition is incremental — both runtimes coexist until Claude Runner can be retired
Notifications are injected in-process via InboundChannel without CLI subprocess overhead
Frontend distinguishes Norn from Claude Runner sessions with runtime badge and correct model display
non_goals: Retiring Claude Runner — this cluster wires Norn in alongside it
Changing the wake prompt format — the delivery mechanism changes, not the content
MCP server integration — Norn tools are in-process, MCP is external-facing
Norn TUI for server sessions — server sessions are headless
Migrating existing Claude Runner session history to Norn format
Real-time tool permission prompting — server sessions are headless, permission is profile-configured
boundaries: SHALL NOT modify the norn crate's Tool trait — namespace tools implement the existing interface
SHALL NOT add MCP transport for internal tools — in-process calls only
SHALL NOT change the wake prompt format
SHALL NOT create a meridian_admin tool — former admin actions are redistributed
SHALL NOT bypass CallerContext authorization — all mutations scope to member_id
SHALL NOT remove the CLI notify check hook — it continues to work for Claude Runner
